review_summary: The transcript provides enough detail for two concept questions on CLIP-style contrastive learning without needing a separate clip.

question_bank.question: What is the key extension from SimCLR to CLIP-style training?
choices: It replaces one side of the contrastive pair with text so matching image-text pairs are pulled together
It removes contrastive learning and trains only with reconstruction loss
It keeps only the text encoder and discards the image encoder
It trains on a single image at a time without negatives
question_bank.explanation: SimCLR aligns two augmented views of the same image. CLIP keeps the contrastive idea but aligns an image representation with its matching text representation in a shared space.

transcript_quotes: image encoder and a text encoder

question_bank.question: Why is CLIP-style pretraining especially scalable?
choices: Because large numbers of image-text pairs can be collected from the internet
Because it needs only one class label for the entire dataset
Because the text encoder does not need any training data
Because downstream tasks never need any additional model heads
question_bank.explanation: The approach scales well because naturally paired images and text exist in huge quantities online, giving a broad supervision signal without requiring a manually labeled category for every example.

transcript_quotes: collecting a lot of image text pairs
